title: Site Inspection Report
description: Walk a commercial asset, document operational issues, and produce a concise inspection report with priorities, photos, and follow-up actions.
whyItMatters: Inspection quality shapes everything downstream. If you miss service issues, safety concerns, or recurring defects on site, you will always be managing stale information. A strong inspection report turns observation into action and gives owners confidence that the property is being actively governed.
skillsLearned: Risk spotting across common areas and plant interfaces
Turning site findings into owner-ready communication

steps.title: Plan the inspection route
steps.description: Choose a property type and build a simple route that covers arrival experience, lobbies, amenities, back-of-house areas, loading, plant interfaces, life-safety points, and any vacancy or fitout zones.
steps.tips: Good inspections follow the customer and the building. Think like a tenant, an owner, and a contractor at the same time.

steps.title: Record findings in categories
steps.description: Capture at least 12 findings and tag each one as safety, maintenance, presentation, compliance, tenant-impacting, or capital-planning related. Include why each finding matters, not just what was seen.
steps.tips: Avoid vague notes like 'looks poor.' Write what is wrong, where it is, who feels the impact, and what should happen next.

steps.title: Assign priority and ownership
steps.description: For each finding, assign a priority level, recommended owner, and target timeframe. Distinguish urgent defects from issues that can be grouped into cyclical works or capex planning.
steps.tips: If everything is urgent, nothing is. Use the Daily Priority Stack mindset when assigning importance.

steps.title: Write the report
steps.description: Assemble a short inspection report with headline observations, a table of issues, and a recommendation section for next steps, spend approvals, or contractor follow-up.
steps.tips: Lead with the 3-4 issues that matter most. The reader should know the asset's current condition in under a minute.

deliverables: A one-page site summary with top risks and service issues
A finding register with 12 or more observations
Priority ratings, owners, and target dates for each issue
A short recommendation section for owner or internal review

rubric.description: Findings are specific, relevant, and grounded in real operational impact rather than vague commentary.
Urgent, near-term, and monitor-only items are clearly separated and logically justified.
The final report is concise, structured, and readable by an owner or senior manager without extra explanation.
Each item has a sensible owner and next step, making the report usable as a live management document.
